mnemonics.title: ICT Evolution Timeline
mnemonics.content: FIRST - Foundational (1940s: transistors), Internet (1960s: ARPANET), Social Web (1990s), Today (2000s+: IoT/Cloud) - remember as F-I-S-T

mnemonics.title: Green Computing Principles
mnemonics.content: GREEN - Green manufacturing, Recycling e-waste, Energy efficiency, Efficient cooling, Nurture sustainability

acronyms.usage: Describes the integrated use of computing and telecommunications for information handling
Network of connected devices that share data automatically
Discarded electronic devices requiring special disposal

acronyms.expansion: Information and Communication Technology for Development
acronyms.usage: Application of technology for socioeconomic progress in developing nations
Environmentally responsible use and disposal of computing resources
Complete cost including purchase, operation, maintenance, and disposal of IT equipment

visualTips.visualization: Draw a timeline starting with ENIAC (1945) → Transistors (1947) → Personal Computers (1981) → World Wide Web (1989) → Cloud Computing (2000s) → IoT (2010s)
Create a diagram showing: Manufacturing (resource extraction) → Usage (energy) → Disposal (e-waste) → All pointing to Environmental Impact (carbon footprint)
Draw a circular flow: Design (energy-efficient) → Manufacture (recyclable materials) → Use (power management) → Dispose (recycle/reuse) → Back to Design

analogies.analogy: Like the nervous system transmits signals throughout the body, ICT transmits data across the globe, connecting people, businesses, and governments instantly.
Like a ticking time bomb, improperly disposed e-waste releases toxic substances over time, contaminating soil and water, causing long-term environmental damage.
Just as factories consume raw materials and energy to produce goods, data centers consume electricity to process and store digital 'products' (data and services).
